items.title: Departmental failures found by child protection review of Kumanjayi Little Baby case

children.content: Nearly three months since the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, a review into her family circumstances prior to the tragedy has been handed down.

articles.title: Protests kick off ahead of NT child protection laws debate

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament is set to debate controversial child protection laws, which have been criticised for their potential impact on the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle.

image.altText: A sign saying 'every removal leaves a scar'

gallery.altText: A handmade card lays amongst other floral tributes to Kumanjayi Little Baby.

thumbnail.altText: A handmade card lays amongst other floral tributes to Kumanjayi Little Baby.

items.title: Job system broken for workers, booming for private equity, report says

children.content: A new report argues Australia's employment services system is failing jobseekers, while filling the pockets of private equity firms and millionaires.

image.altText: people queue up outside a Centrelink office

items.title: Teen suspected of stabbing mother to death, injuring sister in Perth

children.content: Police say a teenage boy is suspected to have stabbed his mother to death and seriously injured his 12-year-old sister in an alleged family violence homicide in Perth's south-east.

image.altText: Two police forensic officers and a detective in a suit stand behind police tape near two police vehicles on a suburban street.

items.title: 'Don't go there': Pauline Hanson fires up in Q&A over White Australia policy

children.content: Pauline Hanson angrily denies suggestions her strident opposition to migration by anyone from "radical Islamic countries" amounts to a version of the White Australia policy.

image.altText: Pauline is pictured at a lectern advertising the Leadership Matters event against a blue background.

items.title: Holocaust survivors have PTSD from antisemitic events, commission hears

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion is this week focused on security for the Australian Jewish community.

image.altText: An elderly man in a suit stands in front of a royal commission banner.

items.title: 'Happening to everybody': AI-manipulated images exploited by scammers

children.content: Papua New Guinea prepares new laws to criminalise the malicious use of artificial intelligence, including deepfakes, voice cloning and digital impersonation.

gallery.altText: An AI-generated image showing Jennifer Baing with a group of people holding cash standing behind another pile of cash.

thumbnail.altText: An AI-generated image showing Jennifer Baing with a group of people holding cash standing behind another pile of cash.

items.title: PMOS/PCOS linked to 'significantly higher' stroke, heart attack risk

children.content: Women with polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS, formerly PCOS) have a "significantly higher" risk of cardiovascular disease than those without, a new study suggests.

image.altText: Practitioner is examining patient checking blood pressure and pulsethen talking to young woman during medical visit.

items.title: Surgeon banned after botched bowel op contributes to patient's death

children.content: A six-month ban on Dr Liu-Ming Schmidt, who works across multiple hospitals in southern NSW, comes after 13 patient complaints including a missed cancer diagnosis and a "catastrophic error" that contributed to a patient's death.

image.altText: A woman walking along a pathway

items.title: India steps up response to US, Iran deadly ship strikes

children.content: The Indian government is taking action after 10 sailors were killed and dozens more injured in attacks on commercial cargo ships and oil tankers during the battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: US, Iran trade fire as Trump says Iran will 'pay' for soldiers' deaths

children.content: The United States has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran after identifying two soldiers killed while defending against Iranian missile and drone attacks in Jordan.

image.altText: A young female soldier in army greens in front of a US flag.

articles.title: Iran-backed Houthis declare maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia

children.content: In a statement, the Houthis' military spokesperson Yahya Saree says the group will enforce the blockade against its "criminal Saudi enemy", effective immediately.

image.altText: Yahya Sarea stands in a military uniform speaking at a podium.

gallery.altText: An Indian boy sits while wearing a stripe shirt.

thumbnail.altText: An Indian boy sits while wearing a stripe shirt.

items.title: US to impose 50pc tariffs on most Canadian goods

children.content: The White House says the tariffs will go into effect in 30 days, meaning there is time for negotiations.

image.altText: A bottleshop is shown with a sign saying 'we are no longer selling liquor from the USA' on a shelf of red wine bottles

items.title: ASX falls as oil prices near one-month high on US-Iran escalation

children.content: The Australian share market has fallen after a negative session on Wall Street. Oil prices remain near a one-month high as Yemen's Houthis threaten to impose a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia. See how the trading day unfolds on our blog. Follow live.

image.altText: A man watches the information screens at the ASX

items.title: Diplomatic efforts to keep US from cutting ties with UNHCR begin, sources say

children.content: The Trump administration is said to be weighing cutting ties with the UN refugee agency, prompting a campaign for the US to maintain support.

image.altText: A white banner includes a logo showing a person seeking shelter under a pair of open hands.

items.title: Anthony Albanese on the night in parliament he would rather not relive

children.content: From left-wing roots to "radical centre" prime minister, Anthony Albanese reflects on his 30-year career in Parliament House.

image.altText: Annabel Crabb and Anthony Albanese stand smiling on the porch of an old building

items.title: Ian doesn't want children. He's part of a growing trend among men

children.content: About 15 per cent of Australian men say they don't want children, an increase from 11 per cent in 2005. Ian says his own childhood played a large part in his decision.

gallery.altText: Ian Hendry with dog Maisy at the beach

thumbnail.altText: Ian Hendry with dog Maisy at the beach

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: Why 'narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home

children.content: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

image.altText: a man gesturing as he sits in front of a laptop

items.title: The discipline behind royal wedding cellist's success

children.content: Sheku Kanneh-Mason went viral performing at Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding. He's captivated audiences since and is touring Australia this month.

image.altText: Sheku Kanneh-Mason sits holding a gleaming cello, one hand reaching towards a music stand. There's a grand piano in the room

items.title: Australian EV owners deal with 'range anxiety'. China has almost eliminated it

children.content: You can swap an empty electric vehicle battery in about four minutes in China. It is part of how China has come to dominate the EV industry.

gallery.altText: A young boy poking his head into the open passenger door of a vehicle with yellow seat covers

thumbnail.altText: A young boy poking his head into the open passenger door of a vehicle with yellow seat covers

items.title: Mayor who provided character reference to sex offender resigns

children.content: Wyndham mayor Preet Singh says he showed "poor judgement" but also claims to have faced "abuse and intimidation" over the issue.

image.altText: A man in mayoral robes and a chain gives two thumbs up, while smiling at the camera.

items.title: How authorities failed to heed one woman's reports of WA's worst child rapist

children.content: Maggie Dawkins's efforts to shine a light on the horrific abuses committed by Dennis McKenna against a generation of boys in Katanning are set down in a new book.

image.altText: Dennis McKenna in brown shirt, tie and jacket on the left. On the right a photo of brick buildings.

items.title: Firewood side hustle sparks multi-million-dollar business in four states

children.content: When bushfire and drought robbed farmer Peter Chauncy of his livelihood, he started chopping wood to sell. Forty years later, he runs one of Australia's largest firewood businesses.

gallery.altText: Man with grey hair holding firewood, wearing orange high-vis jacket, pile of firewood behind him
yellow bags of firewood sitting in front of fuel bowser

thumbnail.altText: Man with grey hair holding firewood, wearing orange high-vis jacket, pile of firewood behind him
yellow bags of firewood sitting in front of fuel bowser

items.title: Vaughters says night tests would have saved cycling '25 years of drama'

children.content: A former cyclist who took performance-enhancing drugs, now a team boss at the Tour de France, supports doping tests in the middle of the night at cycling's biggest race.

image.altText: A former pro cyclist speaks into a microphone at a press conference for a documentary at a film festival.

items.title: Broncos winger Josiah Karapani handed $350 fine for drink-driving offence

children.content: Brisbane Broncos winger Josiah Karapani avoids a criminal conviction after pleading guilty to a drink-driving offence.

image.altText: A man in a white shirt flanked by journalists with mics

items.title: 'Sit down!' Joyce's dog interrupts 7.30 interview as he defends Hanson

children.content: The One Nation MP says Pauline Hanson's meeting with far-right British activist Tommy Robinson did not amount to an endorsement of his criminal conduct, rejecting suggestions she had crossed a "red line".
The otherwise serious 7.30 interview was twice interrupted by Mr Joyce's dog, prompting him to yell at it to "sit down".

items.title: Medical clinic waited months to tell patients of data breach

children.content: Patients at a medical clinic in Brisbane's north only found out about the data breach almost three months after it was targeted by hackers.

image.altText: a health clinic exterior with a sunset behind it

items.title: South Australians urged to consider occasional power outages to curb costs

children.content: The driving force behind skyrocketing power prices this century has been investment by grid owners, and sometimes over-investment, but one expert believes it's time to start questioning our priorities if we want to reduce costs.

image.altText: Close of up high-voltage danger zones

items.title: Tiny 'desert' fish found only in Australia narrowly avoids extinction

children.content: With scoops made from milk bottles, volunteers have been working tirelessly to save the red-fin blue-eye, found nowhere else in the world, from extinction.

gallery.altText: man holding up clear container with small fish in it

thumbnail.altText: man holding up clear container with small fish in it

items.title: Weatherald retains Australian Test spot despite middling Ashes returns

children.content: Jake Weatherald is retained in Australia's 13-player Test squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

image.altText: Australia batter Jake Weatherald plays a cover drive while down on one knee during a Test.

items.title: Nicaragua's president extends rule, says country will not hold elections soon

children.content: Daniel Ortega dashes opposition hopes that he might heed their demands for a democratic vote, effectively cancelled Nicaragua's next elections.

image.altText: Nicaragua's President Daniel Ortega holds a microphone. Beside him his wife, Vice-President Rosario Murillo, claps

items.title: Stubborn stigma fuelling shocking violence against sex workers in Australia

children.content: A new inquiry into violence against sex workers in Australia and Thailand comes just weeks after the murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla, allegedly by an Australian man.

gallery.altText: a woman carries a sign saying 'stop mythologising men who kill sex workers'
A frame of a young Thai girl looking at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: a woman carries a sign saying 'stop mythologising men who kill sex workers'
A frame of a young Thai girl looking at the camera.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes British PM, provides AUKUS hints in first speech

children.content: Andy Burnham has pledged to provide immediate cost-of-living relief for Brits and invited US President Donald Trump to Manchester in the hours after becoming UK PM on Monday.

image.altText: A man in a suit speaking.

items.title: Tate brothers face court for first time since Miami arrest

children.content: Andrew and Tristan Tate's attorney says an attempt to extradite the brothers following their arrest in Miami is politically motivated.

image.altText: Tristan and Andrew Tate pictured sitting down in the audience at an event with serious expressions

items.title: 'Our friends': How a nationwide Bug Hunt galvanized citizen scientists

children.content: More than 1 million observations of insects, spiders, snails and other invertebrates have been recorded by citizen scientists as part of Bug Hunt Australia since October 2025.

gallery.altText: Woman in broad-brimmed hat with a small black beetle on her finger.
A fly up close on a white and green flower.
Isabel and mum erica stand outdoors they are citizen scientists who often take part in the bug hunt

Isabel and mum erica stand outdoors they are citizen scientists who often take part in the bug hunt
